steps.title: Find the key contrast in the passage
steps.content:
The passage says the gas clouds “cannot be touched,” then pivots with “but” to say Leila Mensah believes they “need not remain completely ______ to researchers.” The blank must name the same property as “cannot be touched” — the quality that her 3D-printed models overcome.
  • Choice A — regrettable: Means worthy of regret. The passage does not say the formations are lamentable; it says they are untouchable. ✗
  • Choice B — explicable: Means able to be explained. Nothing in the passage suggests the formations are currently unexplained or confusing. ✗
  • Choice C — intangible: Means unable to be touched or physically grasped — exactly what “cannot be touched” establishes. Mensah’s interactive models make them tangible. ✓
  • Choice D — repeatable: Means able to be done again. Repetition has nothing to do with the passage’s focus on physical accessibility. ✗

steps.title: Confirm with the passage logic
steps.content: The sentence sets up a parallel: gas clouds are untouchable by nature, yet the blank says they need not stay that way — because Mensah’s 3D models give researchers something they can physically handle. Only “intangible” captures both the problem (cannot be touched) and its solution (interactive models). That matches Choice C — intangible.
